# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys directly to production when code is pushed to the main branch. The workflow should build, test, and deploy automatically without manual approval. This enables our continuous deployment workflow for rapid iteration.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD to Production
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  deploy_to_production:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js (example for a Node.js project)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build project
        run: npm run build

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Deploy to Production
        env:
          # Example: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          # Example: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          # Example: PRODUCTION_SERVER_IP: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_SERVER_IP }}
          # Example: SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          # Add any necessary secrets for your deployment method
        run: |
          echo "Deploying application to production environment..."
          # Replace with your actual deployment command(s)
          # Examples:
          #   - Deploy to AWS S3: aws s3 sync ./build s3://your-production-bucket --delete
          #   - Deploy to Heroku: heroku deploy --app your-production-app
          #   - Deploy via SSH: rsync -avz --exclude '.git' ./build user@${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_SERVER_IP }}:/var/www/html/your-app/
          #   - Docker build and push:
          #     docker build -t your-registry/your-app:latest .
          #     docker push your-registry/your-app:latest
          #     kubectl apply -f k8s/production-deployment.yaml
          echo "Deployment successful!"
